comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers around the teebot.dev service, with the creator explaining its simple purpose of printing text on shirts, while others express skepticism about pricing and product details. Some users question the relation to the 'tee' shell command and inquire about print-on-demand suppliers.

comments.author: foxpress
comments.text:
i built teebot.dev because every time i wanted to put text on a shirt, i got lost in some enshitified print companies design flow. i just wanted to get words on a shirt.

So i made teebot.dev. type your text and ship it. that's it.

here's the CLI:

npx teebot "wtf?"
comments.analysis: As the creator, this comment provides a clear and accurate explanation of the product's intent and usage.

comments.author: TipsForCanoes
comments.text: Banner says "custom tees, delivered at cost" but then the preview page shows a price that is double what I get going to their drop shipper directly (printful). No detailed product info on shirt type or quality. No company info. Hard pass.
comments.analysis: Raises valid concerns about pricing transparency and product details, which are important for consumer trust.

comments.author: kleiba
comments.text: Btw, this has nothing to do with the "tee" shell command.
comments.analysis: Clarifies a potential misconception about the product name, helping to set correct expectations.

comments.author: theturtletalks
comments.text: What print on demand supplier are you using? I'm building a merch builder website for startups and most PODs (Gelato, Printify, Printful) all have their own issues.
comments.analysis: Asks a relevant question about the backend supplier, indicating experience with POD platforms and their challenges.
